title: Oxygen Transport (Krogh Cylinder)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Oxygen Transport (Krogh Cylinder): Krogh cylinder model inversion: estimate oxygen diffusivity D and consumption rate Q from tissue pO2 measurements. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.pde.krogh_cylinder…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~50; non_uniform_capillary_spacing, convection_neglect set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: D * nabla ** 2 C - Q(C) = 0 (steady state); Q = Q_max*C/(C+K_m) (Michaelis-Menten)
E.world_state_x: oxygen diffusivity D_O2 and maximum consumption rate Q_max in tissue
E.observation_y: pO2 profile C(r) measured by fiber-optic oxygen probe at radial positions

W.regime: Existence of the recovered oxygen_distribution_field is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 50); non_uniform_capillary_spacing dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: pO2 profile within 5 mmHg for calibrated model. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~50. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |non_uniform_capillary_spacing|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_radial_nodes) per steady-state solve per iteration
